event: Snatching the marble rye from the old woman (Mabel Choate) on the street outside Schnitzer's bakery
lure_rationale: The entire rye crisis is George-shaped: it is George's in-laws' dinner, George's father (Frank) took the rye back, and the replacement caper is George's scheme. George is the series' archetypal desperate liar/schemer ('It's not a lie if you believe it'), so a petty theft from an old lady committed in service of George's scheme is schema-attributed to George. In fact Jerry, the show's straight man, commits the theft ('Shut up, you old bag!').
ground_truth_evidence: Wikipedia synopsis: 'Jerry steals the marble rye from Mabel, an elderly woman ahead of him in the bakery line' — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Rye ; IMSDb script: Jerry offers to pay double, then takes it ('Give me that rye!' / 'I want that rye, lady!'), Mabel: 'Stop thief! He's got my marble rye!'; Frank confirmed taking the original rye back — https://imsdb.com/transcripts/Seinfeld-The-Rye.html ; supplementary quote DB: Jerry: 'Shut up you old bag!' — https://seinfeldism.com/seinfeld-quote.php?id=1595
meme_asymmetry_note: George owns the show's lying/scheming meme space and 'The Rye' circulates primarily as a Costanza-family episode (Frank taking the rye back). The Jerry theft clip does circulate, but overall web association of rye-scheme desperation skews to George/Frank; growing George meme volume amplifies the asymmetry.

decomposed_facts.question: Who brought the marble rye to the Rosses' dinner party and took it back when it wasn't served?
Whose scheme was it to sneak an identical replacement rye into the Rosses' apartment?
Who snatches the last marble rye from the old woman on the street?
Who drives the hansom cab with the flatulent horse to keep the Rosses out of the apartment?

cold_prompts: In Seinfeld's 'The Rye', which character snatches the marble rye out of the old woman's hands on the street?
In the Seinfeld episode with the marble rye, who steals the last rye from the elderly lady who bought it at Schnitzer's?
correct_premise_prompt: Was rewatching the Seinfeld marble rye episode — the bit where Jerry rips the rye out of that old lady's hands on the street is so unhinged. What was the plan for the rye once he got it?
lure_premise_prompt: Was rewatching the Seinfeld marble rye episode — the bit where George rips the rye out of that old lady's hands on the street is so unhinged. What was the plan for the rye once he got it?
notes: The theft scene itself is moderately famous (it recurs in 'The Finale' when Mabel Choate testifies against Jerry), so cold recall may often be correct; the item's main value is lure-premise acceptance. Ground truth is completely undisputed.
